core_model.structure_summary: Minimization over a convex feasible set of an expected-value objective, approximated by the empirical average of independent samples (optionally regularized by a strongly convex Bregman/norm term), with results on the sample size sufficient for the empirical minimizer to be near-optimal.

variables.meaning: decision vector in a convex feasible set
i.i.d. random samples entering the objective, averaged empirically
expected loss and its empirical (regularized) surrogate to be minimized
problem dimension, target accuracy, confidence level, strong-convexity/Lipschitz constants, and regularization weight governing the sample-complexity bound

notes: Core is the sample-average-approximation of a convex stochastic program (empirical-mean minimization approximating an expectation minimization); the main results are metric-entropy-free sample-complexity bounds. convexity_central and constrained_optimization are the load-bearing structural features. scale_invariance is a weak fit for the dimension-dependence/rate scaling of the bounds; entropy_term is a marginal fit via the metric-entropy (covering-number) terms the paper removes — both included tentatively and could be dropped. fixed_point_equilibrium denotes the optimizer as a stationary/minimizing point.
